advantages: Uniquely designed, inner plug adjustability means more precise fixation and suture tensioning of final repair
Reduced suture slippage means less gap formation and rotator cuff displacement
Exclusive tap-in anchor design with offset barbed geometry threads means strong pullout and easy insertion
Unique barbed anchor design provides optimal pull-out strength for a tap-in anchor design
captures suture limbs from neighboring anchors, creating a suture pattern that provides compression of the rotator cuff tissue to the humeral head
adjustability of the suture tension across the surface of the cuff tissue, even after the traditional fixation is completed
internal anchor plug securing the suture can be reversed, allowing an increase or decrease in tension

360º subcortical radial expansion leading to best-in-class anchor fixation as compared to competitive devices in fixation/pull-out benchtop testing

key_features: two osteoconductive components (ß-TCP and calcium sulfate)
advantages: Mechanically stable for a minimum of six months before being absorbed and replaced by bone within 24 months
Two osteoconductive components (ß-TCP and calcium sulfate) acting during different stages of bone healing — no other biocomposite material can claim this
Changes to bone earlier compared to competitive biocomposite materials
Faster, more complete absorption than Arthrex's biocomposite material — 70% absorption of HEALICOIL REGENESORB at 18 months vs 57% for the PLLA/ß-TCP-based Arthrex BioComposite Corkscrew FT (p<0.001)
Open architecture allows bone marrow stem cells to reach the bone-tendon interface to promote healing
Bone grows across the coils of the vented anchor

key_features: derived from highly purified bovine Achilles tendon
advantages: Stimulates the body's natural healing response to support new tendon growth and disrupt disease progression
Gradually absorbs within 6 months and leaves a layer of new tendon-like tissue to biologically augment the existing tendon
Induction of new tendon-like tissue in all patients (N=33)
Mean increase of tendon thickness of 2.2 mm (P < 0.0001) at 3 months
No foreign body/inflammatory reaction
No implant-related complications
High patient satisfaction (94%) after 1 year
Rapid recovery: average 23 days of sling time
